summary: bctx TypeScript evaluation on excalidraw — whole-product proof that bctx is not Python-specific. Numbers measured with bctx v0.1.32 on a pinned excalidraw checkout; honest fidelity labeling.
clone_command: git clone --filter=blob:none https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw.git && cd excalidraw && git checkout 51ca8abde450e44f8f0db1b2708e0408915c7ab1
measurement_note: All numbers reproduce exactly at the pinned commit with bctx 0.1.32. Read every savings number WITH its fidelity. Fidelity = bctx identifier-coverage metric (how much of the original content survives). Token-weighted where aggregated.

packages/element/src.signatures_zero_fallback_files: 6 of 47 (barrel index.ts, types.ts + large files the TS outline parser skips); already included in the token-weighted averages

multi_file_feature_read.note: The number that matters: 5 files an agent opens to understand the 'text element' feature, via the exact `bctx read` path (blueprint/chisel/parallax emit the same outlines).

signatures_LOSSY.fidelity_note: structure only, ~20% fidelity — for understanding/navigation, not editing

per_file_signatures_read_lossy.packages/element/src/linearElementEditor: 18328 -> 18328 tokens (0% — HONEST fallback: this file's TS constructs are skipped by the signatures parser)

command_output_compression.note: Automatic shell-hook compression of noisy git output, recorded to `bctx gain`. Run with an isolated HOME so it doesn't touch your real ~/.bctx.

git_diff_HEAD~40_HEAD.tolerance: before-token count may vary ~1% with local git version/config; savings stays 99%

code_search.note: bctx search is a RELEVANCE tool (ranked, symbol-aware across the whole indexed repo), NOT a token-savings tool. For a narrow literal string, grep output is smaller. No token-savings percentage is claimed for search.
code_search.behavior: returns ranked file::symbol locations with relevance scores

memory.note: Concrete, cross-process: an agent stores a fact via the `sediment` MCP tool; `bctx recall <query>` reads it back from the terminal. Confirmed round-trip.

honest_headline: Understanding an excalidraw feature (5 files as outlines) costs 97% fewer tokens (25217->844, lossy structural). High-fidelity entropy mode: ~34% fewer keeping ~89% content, consistent across dirs. Lossy signatures outline: ~63-76% token-weighted (~20-29% fidelity). Automatic git command compression: 98-99%. Same honest bands as the Python/FastAPI eval — bctx is not Python-specific.

install_paths_verified_v0_1_32.homebrew: brew tap better-ctx-org/bctx && brew install bctx -> 0.1.32
install_paths_verified_v0_1_32.npm: npm install -g bctx-bin -> 0.1.32 (published package correct; local repo package.json cosmetically stale at 0.1.22)
